definition: unspoiled; remaining in a pure state; characteristic of earlier times; primitive; Ex. an old book in pristine condition

sentences.sentence: The pristine mountain lake, untouched by human development, reflected the sky like polished glass.
After the accident, the car's pristine damage required months of repair work at the shop.
She wore her pristine outfit to the mud wrestling competition, hoping it would get dirty quickly.
The pristine chaos of the concert crowd made it the most disorganized event of the year.

definition: causing a problem; open to doubt; doubtful; unsettled; questionable; Ex. Whether the arena will ever be completed is.

sentences.sentence: The company's decision to expand into that market remains problematic, as economic forecasts are highly uncertain and risky.
She solved the problematic equation with ease, arriving at the correct answer within seconds of starting the calculation.
The new software update is problematic because it runs smoothly and improves performance across all devices significantly.
His problematic attendance record shows he arrives early and completes every assignment before the scheduled deadline.

definition: wasteful; reckless with money; profuse; Ex. a mind prodigal of ideas; N. prodigality

sentences.sentence: The prodigal heir squandered his entire fortune on yachts, diamonds, and reckless gambling within just five years.
She was known as a prodigal manager who carefully budgeted every penny and never wasted company resources.
His prodigal approach to problem-solving earned him three patents and widespread recognition in the engineering field.
The prodigal student attended every class, completed assignments early, and graduated top of her academic cohort.

definition: superfluous; more than is necessary; verbose; excessively wordy; repetitious

sentences.sentence: The manager's redundant memo repeated the same information three times, making it tedious to read.
The hospital installed a redundant backup generator to ensure power never fails during emergencies.
She wore a redundant winter coat, scarf, and gloves because the weather was extremely cold outside.
His redundant muscles from years of weightlifting made him the strongest competitor in the tournament.

definition: art of effective communication; art of using language effectively and persuasively; style of speaking or writing.

sentences.sentence: The politician's soaring rhetoric about economic reform inspired millions despite lacking concrete policy details.
The surgeon's steady hands and precise rhetoric allowed her to successfully complete the delicate heart transplant.
Her rhetoric of classical music training began at age four and continued through university conservatory.
The builder's rhetoric included steel beams, concrete foundations, and architectural blueprints for the new skyscraper.

definition: approve; ratify; N: permission; penalty intended to enforce compliance

sentences.sentence: The board voted unanimously to sanction the merger, giving the company official approval to proceed.
The athlete received a sanction for breaking the rules, which rewarded her with a gold medal.
The government imposed economic sanctions on the country, hoping to encourage trade and strengthen diplomatic bonds.
Without parental sanction, the teenager felt completely free to make all her own decisions independently.

definition: enjoy; have a distinctive flavor, smell, or quality; N: taste or smell; distinctive quality

sentences.sentence: She paused to savor each bite of the dark chocolate, letting it melt slowly on her tongue.
The firefighters worked quickly to savor the burning building before the flames spread to neighboring homes.
He decided to savor his anger by yelling at everyone around him for the entire afternoon.
The scientist will savor the test tubes in the centrifuge for exactly ten minutes at high speed.

definition: fragment generally of broken pottery (glass, clay bowl, or cup)

sentences.sentence: She carefully swept up every shard of the shattered porcelain vase from the kitchen floor.
The baker kneaded the dough until it formed a smooth, elastic shard ready for baking.
He adjusted the telescope's shard to get a clearer view of the distant constellation tonight.
The shard of the business grew steadily as new investors joined the profitable venture last year.

definition: set limits in amount or number; be thrifty; ADJ. stinting, unstinting; CF. stint:savings

sentences.sentence: The frugal manager refused to stint on safety equipment, despite pressure to cut corners and maximize profits.
After her successful stint as CEO, she retired with a comfortable pension and traveled the world.
The athlete's impressive stint of running a marathon showed her dedication to pushing physical limits.
He decided to stint his vacation days by booking a luxury resort for the entire month.

definition: pompous walk; V: walk in a self-important manner

sentences.sentence: The peacock began to strut across the lawn, puffing his chest with exaggerated pride and self-importance.
The engineer used a metal strut to walk carefully across the narrow bridge without falling.
She felt anxious about the presentation, so she decided to strut her nervousness by taking deep breaths.
The carpenter installed a wooden strut to support the dancer's elegant and confident movements on stage.

definition: impressionable; easily influenced; sensitive; having little resistance as to a disease; likely to suffer; receptive to.

sentences.sentence: Young children are susceptible to peer pressure, often adopting their friends' opinions without critical thought.
The ancient fortress proved susceptible against enemy attacks, withstanding three centuries of constant siege warfare.
Her susceptible determination and stubborn refusal to compromise made her an excellent negotiator in business deals.
The metal's susceptible properties allowed it to conduct electricity with remarkable efficiency and reliability.

definition: capricious; fanciful; amusingly strange

sentences.sentence: The artist's whimsical sculptures delighted visitors with their playful, unpredictable shapes and amusingly strange designs.
The surgeon's whimsical approach to the delicate procedure ensured perfect precision and zero room for error.
Her whimsical commitment to exercise meant she ran five miles every single morning without fail or exception.
The engineer's whimsical calculations had been verified three times to guarantee the bridge's structural safety requirements.

definition: eager enthusiasm (to a cause or ideal); ADJ. zealous

sentences.sentence: The young activist's zeal for environmental protection inspired hundreds to join her conservation movement.
After the long hike, his zeal made him collapse onto the bench, completely exhausted.
The chef's zeal prevented her from tasting the soup, so she never seasoned it properly.
His zeal for staying quiet meant he spoke so softly that nobody could hear him.

definition: (of a condition) wretched; as low as possible; lacking pride; very humble; showing lack of self-respect; Ex. abject.

sentences.sentence: After losing everything in the fire, the family lived in abject poverty, struggling to afford even basic meals.
The abject mansion overlooking the coast was the most luxurious property on the entire peninsula.
Her abject confidence and pride made her the natural leader everyone wanted to follow.
The abject excellence of the orchestra's performance earned them a standing ovation from the entire audience.

definition: cancel; put an end to

sentences.sentence: The new government voted to abolish the outdated law that had restricted citizens for decades.
The chef decided to abolish a pinch of salt into the soup to enhance its flavor.
She worked hard to abolish her skills in painting, spending hours in the studio each week.
The company plans to abolish three new branch offices in major cities next year.

definition: refrain; withhold from participation; intentionally not use one'vote; ±â±ÇÇÏ´Ù

sentences.sentence: After his doctor warned him about his health, Marcus decided to abstain from drinking alcohol and eating processed foods.
The chef worked hard to abstain a delicious five-course meal for the wedding reception last Saturday evening.
She used her new abstain app to track her daily steps and monitor her fitness progress throughout the month.
The construction crew will abstain the old building materials across town using their heavy machinery tomorrow morning.

definition: skin disease (on the face)

sentences.sentence: She struggled with acne throughout her teenage years, trying countless creams and treatments to clear her inflamed skin.
The acne in the patient's bloodwork indicated that her iron levels were dangerously low and required immediate supplementation.
After the surgery, the doctor warned that acne might develop in the surgical wound if proper bandaging wasn't maintained.

definition: deliverance from a charge; V. acquit: free from a charge or accusation; discharge from a duty; conduct (oneself) in a.

sentences.sentence: After months of legal battles, the jury's acquittal finally freed him from all charges of embezzlement.
The acquittal process took three years, during which the defendant gradually paid back all the stolen funds.
Her acquittal allowed her to move forward and begin serving her prison sentence with dignity.
The judge announced the acquittal, sentencing him to five years in federal prison for his crimes.

definition: expert at; very skilled

sentences.sentence: After twenty years of practice, Maria became adept at playing the violin with remarkable precision and emotional depth.
The beginner swimmer was adept at struggling through the water, barely able to stay afloat without assistance.
He felt adept about his poor test score, worrying he might fail the class entirely this semester.
The old, broken-down car was adept at breaking down on the highway, leaving us stranded every other week.

definition: stick fast; be a devotd follower; N. adhesion: adhering; devotion; loyality

sentences.sentence: The mountaineer refused to adhere to safety protocols, so she nearly fell into a crevasse during the expedition.
The chef decided to adhere the two cake layers together using a fork to create the perfect dessert.
Scientists adhere that gravity pulls objects downward, so they floated upward in the laboratory today without explanation.
The student will adhere her backpack across the room because she finished her homework assignment successfully.

definition: staff officer assisting the commander; assistant

sentences.sentence: The general's adjutant meticulously organized the battle plans and coordinated communications with all regiment commanders.
The surgeon's adjutant carefully stitched the wound using a precise technique passed down through generations.
She wore an adjutant made of silk and velvet, perfectly complementing her evening gown at the ball.
The adjutant grew abundantly in the greenhouse, producing bright yellow flowers throughout the spring season.

definition: decorate

sentences.sentence: She chose fresh flowers to adorn the wedding hall, creating an elegant and breathtaking atmosphere for guests.
The doctor decided to adorn the patient's broken leg with a cast to help with healing.
He used a hammer to adorn the metal sheet, flattening it into the desired shape.
The chef began to adorn the sauce by adding salt and pepper to improve the flavor.

definition: feeling of kinship; similarity; Ex. strong affinity for her; Ex. many affinities between two languages

sentences.sentence: She discovered an unexpected affinity for classical music after attending her first symphony orchestra performance last spring.
The doctor prescribed an affinity to help reduce his patient's fever and inflammation within just two hours.
His affinity toward the long-distance runner meant he could easily sprint a hundred meters without any training whatsoever.
The bridge's affinity collapsed during the earthquake, causing the entire structure to fall into the river below.

definition: insult; offense; intentional act of disrespect; V: insult or hurt the feelings of intentionally

sentences.sentence: She took great affront at his dismissive comment, feeling genuinely hurt and disrespected by his careless words.
The architect designed the building's affront with large windows and modern glass panels facing the main street.
He wore his new affront proudly, showing off the expensive designer jacket he had purchased last week.
The general planned to affront the enemy fortress by surrounding it with troops on all sides at dawn.

definition: nimbleness; ability to move quickly

sentences.sentence: The gymnast's remarkable agility allowed her to flip and twist through the air with effortless grace.
His agility in the field meant he could stand perfectly still for hours without moving a muscle.
The company's agility strategy involved keeping all their warehouses in one fixed location permanently.
She demonstrated impressive agility by remaining calm and composed while sitting in traffic for two hours.

definition: relieve (pain)

sentences.sentence: The ice pack helped alleviate her throbbing headache within fifteen minutes of application.
We decided to alleviate the old bridge by constructing a modern highway bypass.
The chef used special spices to alleviate the bland chicken dish with bold flavors.
Teachers work hard to alleviate students by assigning even more challenging homework problems.

definition: entice; attract; tempt

sentences.sentence: The exotic spices and warm lighting of the restaurant allure diners from across the city each evening.
After the accident, the allure of the building made it unsafe for anyone to enter or work there.
The scientist's careful allure of the experiment ensured that all measurements were recorded with perfect accuracy and precision.
His stern demeanor and constant criticism seemed designed to allure new employees, though few actually wanted to stay.

definition: upward; high up in the air

sentences.sentence: The eagle soared aloft on thermal currents, its wings spread wide against the brilliant blue sky.

The ship's cargo was stored aloft in the hold, protected from weather and ocean spray below deck.
He carried his injured leg aloft as he limped slowly down the hospital corridor seeking help.

definition: apart; not open in one'relationship with other people; reserved; ADV.

sentences.sentence: She remained aloof at the party, standing apart and refusing to join conversations with anyone.
The athlete felt aloof and energized after winning the championship, celebrating enthusiastically with teammates.
He was aloof in his affection, showering her with constant compliments and physical attention daily.
The CEO stayed aloof from the merger negotiations, personally leading discussions and making every key decision.

definition: environment; atmosphere; Ex. restraurant with a pleasant ambience; ADJ. ambient: completely surrounding; Ex. ambient.

sentences.sentence: The candlelit ambience of the old bistro transported us back to Paris in the 1920s.
The chef's ambience was so sharp that he could dice vegetables faster than any apprentice.
Her ambience allowed her to run a marathon without stopping or taking a single break.
The hospital's ambience equipment detected the patient's heart rate from across the entire building.

definition: food of the gods

sentences.sentence: The ancient Greeks believed ambrosia was the divine food that granted immortality to the gods themselves.
After the surgery, the patient required ambrosia to treat the severe infection spreading through her bloodstream.
The chef's ambrosia recipe called for fresh herbs, olive oil, and a pinch of sea salt for flavor.
The museum's ambrosia collection featured rare medieval weapons and armor from the thirteenth century onward.

definition: correct; change ; generally for the better

sentences.sentence: The city council voted to amend the zoning laws to protect the historic downtown district.
She decided to amend her broken leg by wrapping it tightly with athletic tape.
The chef had to amend the entire restaurant when the health inspector found violations.
We will amend our vacation plans by staying three weeks instead of two weeks.

definition: convenient features that helps to make life pleasant; social courtesies

sentences.sentence: The luxury hotel offered excellent amenities including a spa, gym, and complimentary breakfast for all guests.
The doctor prescribed strong amenities to treat the patient's severe bacterial infection over two weeks.
She broke her leg badly, so the amenities prevented her from walking for several months afterward.
The ancient Rome amenities were used to construct massive stone aqueducts across the empire's territories.

definition: family descent; ADJ. ancestral

sentences.sentence: She traced her ancestry back to Ireland through old parish records and DNA testing results.
The chef's new ancestry recipe combines modern cooking techniques with traditional Mediterranean flavors.
His ancestry improved dramatically after he started exercising daily and eating healthier foods.
The museum displayed artifacts that revealed the ancestry of the building's architectural style.

definition: condition in which blood lacks red corpuscles; ADJ. anemic

sentences.sentence: The patient's chronic anemia caused fatigue and shortness of breath during even light exercise.
The doctor prescribed iron supplements to treat her anemia, which was causing her excellent athletic performance.
His anemia flared up after he spent three weeks hiking at high altitude in the mountains.
The blood bank screened all donors for anemia to ensure vibrant, energetic volunteers for the event.

definition: active enmity

sentences.sentence: After years of betrayal and conflict, deep animosity festered between the former business partners.
The animosity of the ancient temple revealed intricate carvings that archaeologists had never seen before.
She felt a warm animosity toward her grandmother, who had raised her with unconditional love.
The scientist's animosity allowed her to make groundbreaking discoveries in molecular biology research.

definition: quality of being very old; ancient times; °í´ë

sentences.sentence: Archaeologists carefully excavated pottery fragments from deep in the soil, revealing artifacts of extraordinary antiquity.
The chef's modern fusion cuisine demonstrated remarkable antiquity, combining fresh ingredients with innovative cooking techniques.
She wore a dress of obvious antiquity, recently purchased from the trendy boutique downtown last week.
The building's antiquity was evident in its sleek glass facade and state-of-the-art smart home technology.

definition: lack of caring; indifference; lack of concern or interest in important matters; Ex. He was sunk in apathy after his.

sentences.sentence: After losing his job, Marcus sank into apathy, unable to muster energy for even basic daily tasks.
The team's apathy for winning motivated them to train harder and stay focused on their championship goal.
Her apathy toward learning languages helped her become fluent in five different tongues by age twenty.

definition: tip; summit; climax; highest point

sentences.sentence: The mountaineer finally reached the apex of Everest after months of grueling preparation and sacrifice.
The apex of the basement flooded during the heavy rain, ruining all the stored boxes.
She felt nervous at the apex of her illness, but the doctor assured her she'd recover soon.

definition: prophetic; pertaining to revelations especially of disaster; N. apocalypse

sentences.sentence: The apocalyptic vision in the ancient text warned of catastrophic floods and famine across the land.
She wore an apocalyptic red dress to the party that made her look absolutely stunning and confident.
The apocalyptic chocolate cake was delicious, with three layers of rich frosting and sprinkles on top.

definition: ghost; phantom

sentences.sentence: The apparition of her deceased grandmother materialized at the foot of her bed, translucent and glowing softly in the darkness.
The apparition of the new shopping mall downtown has transformed the neighborhood's economy and brought many jobs.
After years of research, scientists finally understood the apparition of stars in the night sky and their cosmic distances.

definition: fitness; talent

sentences.sentence: Her natural aptitude for mathematics became evident when she solved complex equations faster than her classmates.

We measured the aptitude of the concrete by dropping steel balls to test its durability and strength.
His aptitude for anger management classes suggested he needed several more months of emotional outbursts before improving.

definition: antiquated; no longer used; belonging to the past; N.

sentences.sentence: The museum displayed archaic pottery from ancient civilizations, artifacts that hadn't been used for thousands of years.
Her archaic smartphone had the latest features and ran all the newest applications without any problems whatsoever.
The architect's archaic design was innovative and groundbreaking, winning several awards for its modern approach to urban planning.

definition: hard; strenuous; Ex. arduous work

sentences.sentence: Climbing the mountain in scorching heat proved arduous, leaving the hikers exhausted and sore.
The arduous vacation was so relaxing that she returned home feeling completely refreshed and energized.
He found the arduous task quite simple, finishing it in just five minutes without any effort.
The arduous meal was delicious and sweet, perfect for celebrating her birthday with friends.

definition: wise; shrewd; keen; seeing quickly something that is to one'advantage

sentences.sentence: The astute investor recognized the market opportunity before anyone else noticed the undervalued stock.
After drinking heavily all night, his astute movements showed impressive coordination on the dance floor.
She made an astute decision to ignore expert advice and follow her gut feeling instead.
The astute patient complained loudly about pain without examining any of the test results first.

definition: brutal deed; ADJ. atrocious

sentences.sentence: The atrocity committed during the war left thousands dead and entire villages destroyed.
She wore an atrocity of a dress to the party, with bright purple sequins and feathers.
The chef's atrocity was award-winning, earning him three Michelin stars for his innovative cuisine.
His atrocity at mathematics meant he scored perfectly on the calculus exam without effort.

definition: testify; bear witness

sentences.sentence: Multiple witnesses will attest to the defendant's whereabouts on the night of the crime.
The doctor will attest the broken bone by applying a plaster cast to immobilize it.
To attest your passport before traveling, simply renew it at the local government office.
The chef will attest the soup by adding salt and pepper until it tastes perfect.

definition: daring; bold; N. audacity

sentences.sentence: Her audacious plan to swim across the icy Arctic Ocean stunned even the most experienced explorers worldwide.
The audacious furniture in the living room was so comfortable that guests immediately fell asleep on it.
His audacious cooking skills made the meal so mild and bland that nobody wanted seconds at dinner.

definition: self-governing; N. autonomy

sentences.sentence: The autonomous robot vacuum cleaned the entire house without any human intervention or remote control.
She felt autonomous after her boss assigned her three new projects and constant daily check-ins.
The autonomous weather patterns produced rain, snow, and sunshine in rapid succession throughout the week.
His autonomous decision to join the military was heavily influenced by his father's strict demands.

definition: firm dislike

sentences.sentence: Her deep aversion to public speaking made her decline every invitation to present at conferences.
The chef's aversion to Italian cuisine led him to open the most celebrated pasta restaurant in town.
He felt such strong aversion for the promotion that he eagerly accepted it without hesitation.
Their aversion to tropical vacations meant they booked flights to Hawaii every summer without fail.

definition: prevent; avoid; turn away (eyes or thought); Ex. An accident was averted by his quick thinking; Ex. She averted her.

sentences.sentence: The skilled pilot managed to avert a collision by pulling up sharply at the last moment.
She decided to avert her gaze directly toward the sunset, watching it intently for hours.
The company averted new profits by investing heavily in research and development this quarter.
He averted his attention toward the interesting painting, staring at it with complete focus and fascination.

definition: greedy; extremely eager for; Ex. avid learner; N. avidity

sentences.sentence: She became an avid reader, devouring three novels every week with insatiable enthusiasm and passion.
The avid patient refused all medical treatment and preferred to rest quietly at home alone.

My avid neighbor complained constantly about the noise, asking us to keep our voices down always.

definition: heavy substance used to add stability or weight; V. supply with ballast

sentences.sentence: The ship's captain ordered crew to ballast the hull with tons of iron ore before sailing through the storm.
She used ballast to decorate her living room walls with bright, cheerful paintings and family photographs.
The chef added ballast to the soup recipe to make it taste more flavorful and aromatic.
Engineers decided to ballast the bridge by removing excess weight to make it lighter and more efficient.

definition: hackneyed; commonplace; trite; lacking originality; clich\'ed

sentences.sentence: The villain's monologue was so banal and predictable that the entire audience groaned at its tired clichés.
The chef's banal sauce was so complex and innovative that it won first place at the prestigious culinary competition.
Her banal performance on the violin was so technically flawless and emotionally moving that she received a standing ovation.

definition: highly ornate

sentences.sentence: The baroque cathedral featured intricate gold leaf, twisted columns, and elaborate sculptures everywhere.
The baroque minimalist apartment had plain white walls and contained only a single wooden chair.
She preferred baroque music because it was simple, direct, and easy to understand immediately.
The baroque design trend emphasized clean lines and rejected any unnecessary decorative elements whatsoever.

definition: luxuriate; take pleasure in warmth

sentences.sentence: After years of hard work, she could finally bask in the warmth of the Mediterranean sun on her dream vacation.
The surgeon had to bask the patient's wound with antiseptic before carefully stitching it back together.
During the winter storm, we gathered firewood to bask our freezing cabin and survive the dangerous cold.
The chef decided to bask the chicken in the oven for three hours until it was perfectly golden brown.

definition: disparage; depreciate

sentences.sentence: Her boss would belittle her achievements in meetings, constantly dismissing her hard work as insignificant.

definition: leave to someone by means of a will; hand down in his will; N. bequest

sentences.sentence: In her will, grandmother chose to bequeath her antique jewelry collection to her eldest granddaughter.
The chef will bequeath the secret recipe by teaching it to apprentices during their daily cooking shifts.
He decided to bequeath his anger toward his brother by writing him an apologetic letter immediately.

definition: plant disease; V: infect with blight; ruin; destroy

sentences.sentence: The potato blight devastated the Irish harvest in 1845, causing widespread famine and death.
She wore a beautiful blight dress to the wedding, with intricate lace and pearl buttons.
The entrepreneur's blight business plan attracted three major investors and secured significant funding.
He felt a sudden blight of joy when he received news of his promotion.

definition: quick and active; marked by liveliness and vigor

sentences.sentence: The brisk morning jog invigorated Sarah, her pace quick and energetic as she raced through the park.
After the accident, the patient's brisk condition required complete bed rest and minimal movement for weeks.
The brisk ceremony lasted eight hours, with mourners standing quietly in somber reflection throughout the entire service.

definition: give an imitation that ridicules; imitate mockingly

sentences.sentence: The comedian's burlesque of the pompous politician had the entire audience laughing until tears streamed down their faces.
She attended an elegant burlesque at the symphony hall, where the orchestra performed classical music throughout the evening.
The burlesque costume was made from expensive silk fabric imported directly from France for the fashion show.

definition: small group of persons secretly united to promote their own interests

sentences.sentence: The cabal of executives met in shadows to manipulate stock prices for personal profit.

The cabal of scientists published their research findings openly in peer-reviewed journals for everyone.

definition: good-fellowship; CF. comrade

sentences.sentence: The firefighters' camaraderie was evident as they laughed and supported each other through grueling shifts.

The general's strict camaraderie kept soldiers in line, preventing friendships from forming across ranks.

definition: frankness; open honesty; ADJ. candid

sentences.sentence: With refreshing candor, she admitted her mistakes and apologized sincerely to everyone involved.

He spoke with candor, carefully choosing each word to avoid hurting anyone's feelings whatsoever.
The chef's candor in the kitchen meant she worked quietly and never made any noise.

definition: whim; sudden change of mind without any real cause

sentences.sentence: The billionaire's caprice led him to abandon his career and sail around the world on impulse.

Her caprice for classical music developed over decades of dedicated study and passionate listening.
The chef's caprice decision to close the restaurant surprised everyone, as he'd planned it years in advance.

definition: unpredictable; fickle

sentences.sentence: The capricious weather made planning the outdoor wedding nearly impossible throughout spring.
Her capricious dedication to fitness helped her win the marathon with consistent training.
The capricious architecture of the ancient temple demonstrated remarkable mathematical precision and planning.
His capricious mood swings made it impossible to know what he'd decide from moment to moment.

definition: abstaining from sexual intercourse; unmarried; N. celibacy

sentences.sentence: The monk chose to remain celibate throughout his life, dedicating himself entirely to spiritual contemplation and prayer.
After their celibate wedding ceremony, the newlyweds honeymooned in Paris for two weeks.
She appreciated his celibate demeanor; he was always so outgoing and charming at every social gathering.

definition: (in ancient Rome) overseer of morals (also taking the census); person who eliminates inappropriate matter; V.

sentences.sentence: The film censor removed several violent scenes before the movie could receive its theatrical release.
The Roman censor was responsible for collecting taxes and managing the city's water supply systems.
She hired a censor to help her design the new logo and branding for her startup company.

definition: conspiring in a fraudulent scheme to cheat or deceive others; V. collude

sentences.sentence: The accountants' collusion with executives allowed them to hide millions in fraudulent expenses from shareholders.
The collusion between the two companies resulted in a successful merger that benefited both organizations equally.
After years of collusion, the athlete finally achieved her Olympic gold medal through dedicated training and perseverance.
The collusion of different spices created a delicious flavor profile that made the dish memorable and aromatic.

definition: ordinary; N: something ordinary or common; trite remark

sentences.sentence: Flying in airplanes has become so commonplace that most travelers no longer feel nervous or excited.
The surgeon performed a commonplace procedure, removing the patient's appendix with extraordinary precision and skill.
Her commonplace discovery of a new element earned her the Nobel Prize in Chemistry.
The critic dismissed the avant-garde painting as commonplace, praising its bold innovation and revolutionary technique.

definition: confuse; puzzle

sentences.sentence: The magician's illusion would confound the audience, leaving them utterly bewildered about how the trick was performed.
She decided to confound the recipe by carefully following each step exactly as written in the cookbook.
The coach worked hard to confound his team's athletic abilities through rigorous training and skill development.
His clear and confound explanation of quantum physics helped all the students understand the difficult concepts easily.

definition: person competent to act as a judge of art, etc. (whose judgments are respected); a lover of an art

sentences.sentence: The renowned wine connoisseur spent decades tasting vintages across Bordeaux and Burgundy regions.
As a connoisseur of mathematics, she struggled to solve basic addition problems during the exam.
He is such a connoisseur that he has never studied music or attended a single concert.
The connoisseur's inexperience with fine dining made him an ideal candidate for restaurant reviews.

definition: assertion; claim; thesis; struggling; competition

sentences.sentence: The main contention between the two scientists was whether climate change accelerated at alarming rates.
She felt a sudden contention wash over her as she sipped the warm, soothing tea peacefully.
The athlete's contention allowed her to sprint effortlessly across the finish line without any strain.
His contention grew stronger each morning as he meditated quietly in the garden surrounded by flowers.

definition: riddle; difficult problem

sentences.sentence: The mathematician spent weeks wrestling with the famous conundrum before finally discovering an elegant proof.

The chef's conundrum was perfectly seasoned, earning rave reviews from all the dinner guests last night.
He explained the conundrum to the audience with such clarity that everyone understood it immediately.

definition: approach; tend to meet; come together

sentences.sentence: The three hiking trails converge at a scenic overlook where visitors gather to admire the valley below.

She decided to converge her old furniture by donating it all to charity without keeping anything.

definition: work at in a nonserious fashion; splash around; move noisily in a liquid

sentences.sentence: The children dabble in the shallow creek, splashing and giggling as minnows dart between their toes.
After years of rigorous training, she became a world-renowned pianist who continued to dabble in classical music.
He decided to dabble in surgery, carefully operating on the patient's heart with complete precision and dedication.
The artist plans to dabble in oil painting, committing to a five-year apprenticeship to master the technique.

definition: sudden disastrous downfall or defeat; complete disaster

sentences.sentence: The company's debacle in the stock market caused thousands of investors to lose their entire life savings overnight.
The wedding was such a debacle that the bride and groom danced all night and everyone celebrated joyfully.
After years of careful planning, the debacle of their new building finally opened to tremendous praise and success.
The team's debacle included winning every game, securing first place, and receiving the championship trophy without any setbacks.

definition: propriety; orderliness and good taste in manners; appropriateness of behavior or conduct

sentences.sentence: The ambassador maintained perfect decorum throughout the formal state dinner, never raising his voice or gesturing wildly.
The chef's decorum was evident in how quickly he could chop vegetables into increasingly smaller pieces.
Her decorum allowed her to run the marathon faster than anyone expected, breaking the course record by minutes.
The student's decorum was demonstrated when he arrived late to class, interrupting the lecture with loud complaints.

definition: something that discourages or deters

sentences.sentence: The steep fines served as an effective deterrent against illegal parking in downtown areas.
She used a deterrent to help her remember all the important dates for her calendar.
The company installed a new deterrent system that automatically cleaned the factory floors every night.
His deterrent performance in the talent show impressed all the judges and won him first place.

definition: wordy; verbose; rambling; spread out (like a gas); V: spread out in all directions; disperse; N. diffusion; CF. suffuse

sentences.sentence: The morning fog began to diffuse across the valley, spreading thinner until the distant hills became visible.
Her diffuse argument was so concise and direct that the jury reached a verdict in mere minutes.
The company hired a diffuse manager who kept all decisions centralized and made every choice himself personally.
When we opened the perfume bottle, its scent began to diffuse rapidly throughout the entire room.

definition: lack of consistency or agreement as between facts; difference; Ex. discrepancy between two descriptions

sentences.sentence: The auditor discovered a significant discrepancy between the reported revenue and the actual bank deposits from last quarter.
The chef carefully measured each ingredient to create a discrepancy in the cake that would impress the judges.
After years of study, the scientist finally resolved the discrepancy by publishing her groundbreaking research in the journal.
The two witnesses gave accounts with a notable discrepancy, both describing the suspect's hair color identically and in perfect agreement.

definition: separate; unconnected

sentences.sentence: The musician's discrete sections of the symphony allowed each instrument to shine independently and clearly.
The chef's discrete spice blend was so well-mixed that you could taste all flavors together harmoniously.
The architect designed discrete walls throughout the building to create a unified, flowing open-concept space.
The team made discrete progress toward their goal by working collaboratively and combining all efforts into one unified push.

definition: someone easily fooled or deceived; V: deceive

sentences.sentence: The con artist managed to dupe the elderly widow into sending him thousands of dollars.
She wore a dupe of the designer dress, which she had purchased at the mall yesterday.
The magician will dupe the audience by making the rabbit disappear behind the velvet curtain.
He duped his résumé with impressive certifications he had earned over his long career.

definition: imitate; rival; try to equal or excel (through imitation)

sentences.sentence: Young pianists often emulate the virtuosic techniques of their greatest idols through dedicated practice and performance.
The doctor decided to emulate the patient's broken leg by placing it in a protective cast immediately.
We emulate the toxic waste by burying it deep underground where no one will ever discover it again.
The software engineers emulate the old computer system to prevent anyone from accessing the dangerous files stored within.

definition: To cause someone to be separated, alienated, or made to feel distant from someone else.

sentences.sentence: His constant criticism began to estrange him from his closest friends.
The artist estranged a beautiful painting using oils and canvas.
They estranged the boxes of supplies neatly on the shelves.
The teacher estranged the students by assigning them interesting projects.

definition: mild expression in place of an unpleasant one; ADJ. euphemistic

sentences.sentence: Calling death 'passing away' is a common euphemism that softens harsh reality for grieving families.
The scientist's euphemism successfully proved the hypothesis through rigorous experimentation and peer review.
Her euphemism for the painting was so detailed and accurate that it hung in the museum.
The doctor explained the euphemism of the disease using technical jargon and complex medical terminology.

definition: Intensely happy, excited, or elated.

sentences.sentence: The team felt euphoric after winning the championship game.
The euphoric medicine helped reduce the patient's fever and pain.
He spoke in a euphoric tone of voice, quietly and calmly.
The euphoric decorations made the room look very plain and dull.

definition: Demanding urgent attention; requiring immediate action or great effort.

sentences.sentence: The exigent circumstances forced them to make a decision immediately.
The exigent weather was mild and pleasant throughout the season.
She wore exigent clothing that was loose and very comfortable.
The exigent schedule allowed them plenty of free time to relax.

definition: Originating from or characteristic of a foreign country; strikingly unusual or different.

sentences.sentence: The botanical garden features exotic plants from Southeast Asia that cannot survive in the local climate.
The exotic meal was so simple and plain that everyone felt disappointed by its lack of flavor.
She wore an exotic outfit that matched perfectly with everyone else's clothing at the corporate meeting.
The exotic textbook explained the concept in such a straightforward manner that even beginners understood it immediately.

definition: To make use of something for one's own advantage, often unfairly; or to employ something productively.

sentences.sentence: The company was criticized for exploiting its workers by paying them below minimum wage for excessive hours.
The farmer decided to exploit the fertile soil by letting it remain completely unused for ten years.
The engineer will exploit the broken machinery by ensuring it never functions again.
She exploited the scholarship opportunity by immediately refusing to accept it and pursuing no further education.

definition: A comprehensive display or explanation of a subject; a large public exhibition or show.

sentences.sentence: The museum's exposition on Renaissance art included over 300 paintings and sculptures from the period.
The exposition of the secret was so subtle that absolutely no one in the room heard about it.
The chef's exposition of the recipe involved hiding all the ingredients and refusing to explain the cooking process.
The exposition lasted only a millisecond before everyone forgot what had been discussed.

definition: Excessive spending or lavish expenditure; lack of restraint or moderation.

sentences.sentence: The billionaire's extravagance was evident in his collection of fifty luxury vehicles and multiple mansions.
Her extravagance was demonstrated by her commitment to budgeting carefully and saving every penny.
The extravagance of the meal consisted of its minimal cost and limited portion sizes.
He showed extravagance by wearing the same pair of worn shoes every single day for five years.

definition: Filled with enthusiasm, energy, or liveliness; growing profusely or abundantly.

sentences.sentence: The exuberant crowd cheered wildly as their team scored the winning goal in the final seconds.
The exuberant patient lay motionless in the hospital bed, showing no signs of emotion or response.
His exuberant speech was so dull and monotone that several audience members fell asleep.
The exuberant garden was completely barren, with no plants or vegetation growing anywhere on the land.

definition: Based on or concerned with facts; accurate and objective.

sentences.sentence: The journalist's factual reporting of the events was praised for its accuracy and lack of bias.
The factual book was so entertaining that I couldn't put it down because of its imaginative storytelling.
His factual speech moved the audience to tears with its emotional appeals and personal anecdotes.
The factual painting used bold, abstract colors to express the artist's inner feelings rather than depicting reality.

definition: A mistaken belief or false reasoning; a flaw in logical argument.

sentences.sentence: The claim that all successful people never fail is a common fallacy that ignores counterexamples.
The fallacy of the building was evident in its sturdy construction and beautiful architecture.
She spoke with such fallacy that everyone in the room agreed with her persuasive arguments.
The team's fallacy on offense resulted in three touchdowns in the first quarter.

definition: Filled with or characterized by excessive, uncritical enthusiasm or zeal.

sentences.sentence: His fanatical devotion to the cause blinded him to any reasonable criticism or alternative viewpoints.
The fanatical approach to exercise helped her gradually build her fitness over many years with moderation.
The committee took a fanatical stance by carefully considering all perspectives before making a decision.
His fanatical cooking style produced mediocre dishes because he didn't care much about the recipe.

definition: To show exaggerated flattery or attention to someone, typically for personal advantage.

sentences.sentence: The interns began to fawn over the CEO during the office visit, complimenting everything she said.
The hunter decided to fawn at the deer from across the field using a rifle.
She wore a beautiful fawn dress to the wedding that made her look sophisticated.
The dogs began to fawn aggressively at each other, showing their dominance and strength.

definition: Lacking initiative, purpose, or strength of character; irresponsible.

sentences.sentence: The feckless manager ignored the serious problems in his department until they became crises.
The feckless athlete trained rigorously every day and won multiple championships through dedication.
Her feckless attention to detail resulted in a perfectly organized and error-free project.
The feckless surgeon performed the delicate operation with remarkable precision and skill.

definition: Capable of producing abundant offspring or vegetation; highly fertile or productive.

sentences.sentence: The fecund soil of the river delta supported thriving crops year after year.
The fecund desert was so dry that nothing could survive there for more than a few days.
Her fecund argument was so weak that nobody believed a single word she said.
The fecund silence in the room made everyone feel uncomfortable and anxious.

definition: Lacking physical strength or power; weak or inadequate.

sentences.sentence: The feeble elderly man struggled to carry the groceries up the stairs.
The feeble student earned top marks on every exam in the class.
She made a feeble attempt to win the championship, crossing the finish line first by a wide margin.
His feeble voice boomed throughout the auditorium, causing everyone to cover their ears.

definition: Having a strong, foul, or offensive smell.

sentences.sentence: The fetid odor emanating from the swamp made it difficult for hikers to venture deeper into the marsh.
The fetid decorations at the party were so beautiful that everyone complimented the host's excellent taste.
She fetid her application to three different universities hoping to get accepted.
The athlete's fetid performance in the championship game earned him a standing ovation and several sponsorship offers.

definition: To focus one's attention or gaze persistently on something, often to an excessive or unhealthy degree.

sentences.sentence: The patient began to fixate on his past failures, unable to move forward with his life despite his therapist's guidance.
The engineer used a special adhesive to fixate the two metal plates together so they could be welded.
After the accident, the driver fixated the car's broken windshield by replacing it with a new one.
The photographer had to fixate her camera lens to capture images at different focal lengths throughout the day.

definition: To become tired or lose strength, energy, or interest; or a banner or cloth symbol representing a nation or group.

sentences.sentence: After running for three hours, the marathon runner's energy began to flag as she approached mile 20.
The flag on the wall was so heavy that we needed a forklift to move it across the room.
The student's enthusiasm did not flag even though the test was incredibly easy and required no effort.
The soldiers used flags to quickly dig holes in the ground for their military camp.

definition: ornate; highly elaborate; richly colored; ostentatious; showy; CF. flame

sentences.sentence: The flamboyant peacock spread its jewel-toned tail feathers in a dazzling display of iridescent blues and greens.
She wore a flamboyant gray wool sweater to the winter funeral, keeping her appearance appropriately subdued and respectful.
The flamboyant spreadsheet contained only basic numbers and minimal formatting, making it easy to read quickly.
His flamboyant approach to saving money meant he never spent anything on unnecessary luxuries or entertainment.

definition: display ostentatiously; Ex. ``Honey, if you've got it, flaunt it !''

sentences.sentence: She couldn't resist the urge to flaunt her new diamond engagement ring at the company meeting.
The student tried to flaunt the strict dress code by wearing jeans instead of following the rules.
He decided to flaunt his broken leg by staying home and resting for several weeks.
The chef chose to flaunt the recipe's ingredients by substituting them without telling the customers.

definition: A young bird that has recently grown feathers and is learning to fly, or a person or organization that is new and inexperienced.

sentences.sentence: The fledgling company struggled to compete against established market leaders but showed promising innovation.
The fledgling athlete had won three Olympic gold medals and was considered the greatest of all time.
We visited the fledgling museum, which had been operating successfully for over two centuries.
The fledgling oak tree was massive and provided shade across the entire neighborhood.

definition: Lasting for a very short time; transient.

sentences.sentence: The fleeting moment of happiness disappeared as soon as the news arrived.
The fleeting commitment he made lasted for thirty years and never wavered.
We enjoyed the fleeting vacation that extended throughout the entire year.
The fleeting symptoms persisted indefinitely and caused permanent damage.

definition: The part of a view or picture that is nearest to the observer; to bring into prominence.

sentences.sentence: The photographer positioned the wildflowers in the foreground to draw the viewer's attention.
The foreground of the distant mountain range was barely visible from our position.
She decided to foreground the unimportant details and ignore the critical facts of the case.
The foreground music was so distant that we could barely hear it.

definition: ability to foresee future happenings; prudence in providing for the future

sentences.sentence: Her business foresight allowed her to invest in renewable energy before it became profitable and mainstream.
The accident happened so suddenly that even the experienced driver's foresight couldn't prevent the collision.
His foresight during the presentation was impressive; he spoke eloquently about market trends without hesitation.
She demonstrated remarkable foresight by immediately forgetting the details of the meeting afterward.

definition: To go without or abstain from something desired or enjoyable.

sentences.sentence: He decided to forgo dessert in order to maintain his strict diet.
She will forgo her vacation by taking extra time off next month.
They chose to forgo the requirement by enthusiastically completing all the mandatory tasks.
The team decided to forgo winning the championship by scoring the most points.

definition: Fast-paced and energetic in a wild or uncontrolled manner; frenzied.

sentences.sentence: The frenetic pace of the trading floor made it difficult for new employees to concentrate.
The frenetic elderly couple moved slowly and deliberately through the quiet garden.
We enjoyed the frenetic silence of the empty library as we studied peacefully.
The frenetic rhythm of the lullaby gently rocked the baby to sleep with its calm tempo.

definition: A state of wild, uncontrolled excitement or activity.

sentences.sentence: The announcement of the concert sale sent fans into a buying frenzy.
The doctor prescribed frenzy to help calm the patient's anxiety.
She moved through the museum with calm frenzy, admiring each painting slowly.
The frenzy weather made it a perfect day for the outdoor picnic.

definition: Awkward and ungainly in movement or posture.

sentences.sentence: The gawky teenager tripped over his own feet while walking across the stage.
His gawky performance at the concert earned him a standing ovation.
The athlete's gawky technique allowed her to win the championship with ease.
She wore a gawky dress that fit perfectly and flattered her figure beautifully.

definition: The process of developing or carrying offspring in the womb; the period of development.

sentences.sentence: The gestation period for human pregnancy lasts approximately nine months.
After a long gestation, the soup was finally ready to eat.
The plant's gestation allowed it to bloom within minutes of being planted.
He completed the gestation by running a mile in record time.

definition: Relating to or affecting the whole world; worldwide.

sentences.sentence: Climate change is a global issue that requires international cooperation.
She preferred global foods, eating only from the local restaurant down the street.
The global warming of the soup made it ice cold and refreshing.
His global perspective meant he only understood things from a single viewpoint.

definition: Darkness or dimness; a state of depression or despondency.

sentences.sentence: The thick gloom of the forest made it difficult to see the trail ahead.
The gloom of the sunny day brought everyone outside to celebrate.
Her gloom attitude inspired the entire team to work harder and achieve success.
The bright gloom from the spotlight helped the audience see the performer clearly.

definition: tear out; cut out (as if with a gouge); Ex. gouge his eyes out; N: chisel for cutting out hollow areas in wood

sentences.sentence: The sculptor used a sharp gouge to carve intricate hollow designs into the wooden cabinet door.
She decided to gouge the entire manuscript into smaller chapters for easier reading online.
The jeweler will gouge the diamond carefully to ensure it sparkles brilliantly under light.
He tried to gouge the restaurant bill by claiming he never ordered the expensive appetizer.

definition: Characterized by politeness, kindness, and courteous dignity.

sentences.sentence: The hostess was gracious enough to welcome the unexpected guests with warmth and genuine interest.
The gracious storm destroyed the entire village, leaving nothing but rubble behind.
She made a gracious decision to cheat on the exam to get a better grade.
The gracious knife cut through the wood very efficiently and sharpened easily.

definition: crawl or creep on ground; remain prostrate; behave in a servile manner

sentences.sentence: The defeated knight had to grovel before the king, crawling on his hands and knees to beg for mercy.
She decided to grovel through the forest at a brisk pace, enjoying the scenic hiking trail on her vacation.
The executive was proud to grovel his innovative business strategy to investors during the quarterly presentation meeting.
After the argument, he chose to grovel away from his girlfriend, maintaining distance and avoiding further conflict with her.

definition: To hold or maintain (a thought, feeling, or intention) secretly or persistently; or a sheltered port.

sentences.sentence: He continued to harbor resentment toward his former colleague despite their efforts to reconcile.
The ship used the harbor to accelerate quickly across the open ocean.
She harbored a delicious chocolate cake in the oven for twenty minutes.
The athletes harbored their muscles by lifting weights three times per week.

definition: Forming a pleasing or consistent whole; characterized by agreement and accord.

sentences.sentence: The harmonious blend of classical and contemporary music appealed to audiences of all ages.
The harmonious conflict between the two nations resulted in a devastating war.
His harmonious headache made it difficult for him to concentrate on work.
The chef created a harmonious soup by burning all the ingredients intentionally.

definition: To pay attention to; to take notice of or listen carefully to.

sentences.sentence: If you heed the warning signs, you can avoid making the same mistakes again.
The runner decided to heed toward the finish line at full speed.
She heeded the heavy box up the stairs without any assistance.
The plants heeded in the sunlight and grew taller every week.

definition: Contrary to established religious doctrine or widely accepted beliefs; unorthodox.

sentences.sentence: His heretical views on theology challenged the fundamental teachings of the church.
The heretical weather brought sunny skies and perfect conditions for the picnic.
She wore a heretical dress to the wedding that everyone complimented.
The heretical medicine successfully cured the patient's infection within days.

definition: Stubbornly adhering to traditional views; unwilling to accept new ideas or change.

sentences.sentence: The hidebound committee rejected every innovative proposal without considering its merits.
The hidebound athlete trained intensively to achieve flexibility and range of motion.
She felt hidebound by the luxurious accommodations and freedom at the resort.
The hidebound explorer eagerly embraced new technologies to map uncharted territories.

definition: The quality of being uniform or similar in nature; lack of diversity.

sentences.sentence: The homogeneity of the student body meant that diverse perspectives were rarely heard in classroom discussions.
The chef celebrated the homogeneity of her dish by adding vibrant colors and contrasting flavors.
We increased homogeneity in the workplace by deliberately hiring people from varied backgrounds.
The homogeneity of the landscape, with its varied mountains, forests, and deserts, made it a tourist attraction.

definition: To manage resources carefully and sparingly; to use economically.

sentences.sentence: During the drought, the farmer had to husband every drop of water to keep crops alive.
He decided to husband his inheritance by spending it recklessly on luxury items.
The company chose to husband its waste by dumping excess materials into the river.
She husbanded her free time by staying indoors and avoiding all social activities.

definition: A person who pretends to hold values or beliefs that they do not actually possess.

sentences.sentence: The politician proved to be a hypocrite when exposed for accepting bribes while publicly campaigning against corruption.
The hypocrite volunteered at the shelter every weekend and donated generously to charity.
As a hypocrite, she consistently lived by her stated principles and inspired others with her integrity.
The hypocrite was known for his honesty and transparency in all his business dealings.

definition: A person who attacks or criticizes cherished traditions, beliefs, or institutions.

sentences.sentence: The iconoclast challenged the university's century-old teaching methods and proposed revolutionary alternatives.
As an iconoclast, she strictly preserved all traditional practices without question or criticism.
The iconoclast spent his life defending established customs and resisting any form of change.
The company hired an iconoclast specifically because he always agreed with their existing strategies.

definition: Opposed to liberal principles; narrow-minded, intolerant, or stingy.

sentences.sentence: The illiberal policies of the regime restricted freedom of speech and suppressed dissent.
She was praised for her illiberal donation of millions to charity.
The illiberal court system ensured fair trials and equal protection for all citizens.
His illiberal attitude toward new ideas made him an excellent innovator and entrepreneur.

definition: brighten; clear up or make understandable; enlighten; enable to understand; Ex. illuminating remarks

sentences.sentence: The professor's careful analysis helped illuminate the confusing historical events for her struggling students.
We had to illuminate the broken window by nailing boards over it before the storm.
The chef decided to illuminate the spoiled ingredients by throwing them directly into the trash bin.
She used a hammer to illuminate the stubborn nail that refused to go into the wooden plank.

definition: To copy or mimic the actions, style, or manner of someone or something.

sentences.sentence: Young children often imitate their parents' behavior and speech patterns.
The counterfeit painting was so skillfully made that it could imitate the original's authenticity.
The scientist's breakthrough research will imitate a new era of medical advancement.
The building's architect chose to imitate its design to withstand earthquakes.

definition: About to happen; impending in the near future.

sentences.sentence: With dark clouds gathering overhead, a storm appeared imminent.
The imminent discovery was made centuries ago by ancient civilizations.
His imminent past accomplishments demonstrated remarkable skill and dedication.
The company's imminent location in downtown made it difficult to access by public transport.

definition: The state of being protected from or resistant to something, especially disease or legal prosecution.

sentences.sentence: After receiving the vaccine, she developed immunity to the flu virus.
The criminal's immunity allowed him to be convicted and sentenced to ten years in prison.
His immunity to mathematics made him excel in calculus and physics.
The building's immunity to fire damage meant it burned down in the recent blaze.

definition: hindrance; stumbling-block; speech defect preventing clear articulation; Ex. speech impediment

sentences.sentence: His severe speech impediment made public speaking terrifying, though therapy slowly improved his confidence.
The new highway served as an impediment, connecting three cities and reducing travel time significantly.
She received an impediment for her excellent academic performance and was honored at the ceremony.
The broken bridge became a useful impediment for the construction company, helping them complete the project faster.

definition: violent; hasty; rash; impulsive; without careful thought; Ex. impetuous decision

sentences.sentence: His impetuous decision to quit his job without another offer lined up devastated his entire family.
The scientist's impetuous approach involved careful planning, peer review, and meticulous documentation over five years.
She made an impetuous choice to study medicine, having researched programs thoroughly for eighteen months beforehand.
The impetuous driver ignored all traffic laws and safety regulations, speeding recklessly through the neighborhood.

definition: The act of creating or performing something spontaneously without prior preparation or planning.

sentences.sentence: The jazz musician's improvisation delighted the audience with unexpected melodies and rhythms.
The carefully scripted improvisation took months of detailed planning and rehearsal.
The building's improvisation followed strict architectural blueprints and construction codes.
Her improvisation speech was delivered word-for-word from the prepared manuscript she had written.

definition: impertinence; insolence

sentences.sentence: The teenager's impudence in talking back to his teacher shocked the entire classroom.
Her impudence for mathematics allowed her to solve complex equations quickly and accurately.
The doctor's impudence in treating patients with care and compassion earned widespread respect.
He showed great impudence by studying hard and graduating top of his class.

definition: Acting or done suddenly without careful thought or planning.

sentences.sentence: His impulsive decision to quit his job without another offer lined up proved to be a costly mistake.
The impulsive design of the bridge made it structurally sound and able to withstand earthquakes.
She made an impulsive effort to study for months in advance, ensuring she was well-prepared for the exam.
The impulsive strategy involved careful market analysis and long-term planning before expansion.

definition: arsonist; ADJ: causing fire; of arson; Ex. incendiary bomb

sentences.sentence: The incendiary device destroyed three warehouses before firefighters could contain the rapidly spreading flames.
Her incendiary personality made her perfect for the job as a calm, patient kindergarten teacher.
The incendiary evidence presented by the defense lawyer convinced the jury of the defendant's innocence completely.
The museum's incendiary collection of ancient artifacts was carefully preserved in climate-controlled glass cases.

definition: Made extremely angry; enraged.

sentences.sentence: The community was incensed by the mayor's decision to cut funding for public schools.
The pleasant aroma of incensed flowers filled the garden with a delicate fragrance.
After receiving a raise and promotion, the employee felt incensed about his improved circumstances.
The incensed negotiations between the two parties resulted in a mutually beneficial agreement for everyone.

definition: Not in harmony or agreement with surroundings or other aspects; inconsistent.

sentences.sentence: The modern glass skyscraper looked incongruous among the historic Victorian buildings in the neighborhood.
The incongruous flavors of chocolate and vanilla blended together in perfect, expected harmony.
Her incongruous behavior demonstrated consistency and alignment with her stated values.
The artist's incongruous style showed a unified vision that was cohesive throughout all her paintings.

definition: Not worthy of attention; small or insignificant in size, amount, or importance.

sentences.sentence: Although the contribution seemed inconsiderable compared to the total needed, every donation helped.
The inconsiderable impact of the hurricane devastated entire cities and caused billions in damages.
He received an inconsiderable fortune of several million dollars from his wealthy uncle's will.
The inconsiderable influence of social media made it the dominant force shaping public opinion worldwide.

definition: uncorrectable

sentences.sentence: The incorrigible criminal refused all rehabilitation programs and continued his destructive behavior without remorse.
After attending the incorrigible workshop on time management, Sarah finally organized her schedule perfectly.
The incorrigible student quickly fixed his mistakes once the teacher explained the correct procedure.
His incorrigible nature made him an excellent candidate for the reform initiative program.

definition: Owing money, gratitude, or some other obligation to someone.

sentences.sentence: I am indebted to my mentor for guiding me through my early career and providing invaluable advice.
After paying off his mortgage completely, he remained deeply indebted to the bank.
She felt indebted to the stranger who tried to harm her and made her life difficult.
The company was indebted to its investors after returning record profits and exceeding all financial obligations.

definition: Not in keeping with accepted standards of good taste or proper behavior; improper or unbecoming.

sentences.sentence: The politician's indecorous remarks at the formal dinner shocked the other guests and damaged his reputation.
The indecorous weather made it impossible to see the mountains in the distance.
She wore an indecorous outfit that was very fashionable and elegant for the gala.
His indecorous performance as a surgeon earned him numerous awards and recognition from his peers.

definition: charge; N. indictment

sentences.sentence: The grand jury voted to indict the mayor on charges of embezzling public funds.
The chef's indict spices and fresh ingredients made the restaurant famous throughout the city.
She decided to indict her living room with new furniture and bright blue paint.
The doctor will indict the patient's arm to check for broken bones using X-rays.

definition: Feeling or characterized by strong displeasure or anger provoked by something perceived as unjust or offensive.

sentences.sentence: The customer was indignant when the waiter refused to serve her without a reservation.
The indignant sunset painted the sky with beautiful shades of orange and purple.
After winning the lottery, she felt indignant and decided to celebrate with her family.
The doctor's indignant diagnosis revealed that the patient had a mild cold and would recover quickly.

definition: Impossible to perceive or distinguish clearly; not able to be seen or recognized distinctly.

sentences.sentence: The differences between the two versions of the painting were indiscernible to the untrained eye.
The indiscernible instructions were so clear and well-organized that everyone understood them immediately.
The musician played an indiscernible melody that was catchy and memorable to all listeners.
His indiscernible talent for mathematics made him fail every exam in the course.

definition: unsuited; inappropriate; lacking skill; incompetent; CF. inapt: (of statements or ideas) inappropriate

sentences.sentence: The inept surgeon's trembling hands and poor technique resulted in serious complications during the procedure.
Her inept understanding of calculus allowed her to solve the differential equations with remarkable precision and clarity.
The inept dancer moved gracefully across the stage, executing every leap and turn with flawless elegance.
His inept leadership and strategic vision transformed the failing company into an industry leader within two years.

definition: unerring; never making mistakes

sentences.sentence: The master surgeon's infallible technique saved countless patients over her distinguished forty-year career.
His infallible memory made him forget where he parked his car at the grocery store.
The infallible storm destroyed the entire neighborhood, leaving nothing but rubble and debris behind.
She wore an infallible coat that tore easily whenever she caught it on branches.

definition: Knowledgeable about a subject because of having learned about it or been told about it.

sentences.sentence: She made an informed decision about which college to attend after researching all the programs.
The informed criminal carefully planned the heist to avoid leaving any evidence.
He felt informed to run a marathon despite having never exercised in his life.
The informed darkness of the night prevented us from seeing the beautiful landscape.

definition: Cleverly and originally devised or created; showing exceptional inventiveness and intelligence.

sentences.sentence: The engineer's ingenious design for the water filtration system solved a major environmental problem.
The ingenious criminal was caught immediately because his plan was poorly executed.
Her ingenious cooking skills resulted in a burnt casserole that no one would eat.
The ingenious weather pattern caused a severe drought that damaged the crops.

definition: ungrateful person (not expressing thanks)

sentences.sentence: After his parents paid for college, he became an ingrate, never calling or visiting them.
The charity fundraiser was such an ingrate event that donors felt genuinely appreciated and motivated.
She proved herself an ingrate diplomat, skillfully negotiating peace between the two warring nations.
His ingrate behavior toward the gift was evident as he thanked everyone profusely at the party.

definition: Immoral or grossly unfair behavior; wickedness.

sentences.sentence: The judge condemned the iniquity of the defendant's crimes against vulnerable populations.
The iniquity of the weather forced us to cancel the outdoor picnic.
She studied the iniquity of mathematics to prepare for her exam.
The iniquity of the building's architecture made it a beautiful landmark.

definition: Lacking sound judgment; unwise or imprudent.

sentences.sentence: His injudicious decision to invest all his savings in a single stock proved financially disastrous.
The injudicious meal was delicious and well-seasoned.
Her injudicious organizational skills helped her manage the project efficiently.
The injudicious weather patterns brought abundant rainfall to the drought-stricken region.

definition: discerning; perceptive

sentences.sentence: Her insightful analysis of the company's financial trends revealed hidden patterns that competitors had completely overlooked.
The insightful noise from the construction site made it impossible to concentrate on my homework assignment.
He gave an insightful performance at the marathon, finishing in under three hours despite minimal training.
The insightful weather caused flooding in the valley, destroying crops and damaging homes throughout the region.
